questions: What question about life keeps returning to you in quiet moments?
When have you felt small in a way that changed your perspective?
What do you imagine your legacy will be, even in small terms?
When did you experience a moment that made everything else feel trivial?
What belief about why we're here feels most true to you?
When did you confront your limits and what did you learn?
What do you fear most about the passage of time?
When did you feel connected to something larger than yourself?
What meaning have you found in hardship that surprised you?
When did gratitude and awe appear together in your life recently?
What does 'a well-lived life' look like to you personally?
When did you feel life asking you to choose differently and how did you respond?
What idea about reality would you like to understand better?
When did you notice a pattern that suggests a bigger truth to you?
What small daily practice helps you live with more presence?
When did a loss make you rethink what truly matters?
What hope about the future do you hold onto when things feel uncertain?
When did you feel awe at the ordinary and what prompted it?
What would you ask the version of yourself twenty years from now?
When did you notice your values shifting and why?
What do you wonder about the balance between fate and choice?
When did you feel a sense of belonging to the universe rather than just people?
What role does curiosity play in how you face big unknowns?
When did you feel the comfort of not needing to have all the answers?
What story about your life do you hope remains true at the end?
When did you reconsider a belief that used to feel certain?
What thought about mortality brings you the most clarity?
When did you notice how small acts can hold great meaning?
What question about identity would you ask a trusted friend to explore together?
When did you feel reconciled to a path you once resisted?
What do you think gives life its flavor even in times of routine?
When did you feel awe at human kindness and what was the moment?
What do you imagine is the simplest truth behind the chaos?
When did a conversation shift your sense of purpose?
What quiet wonder helps you get through difficult days?
When did you accept a mystery instead of trying to solve it?
What memory makes you feel both small and fiercely alive?
When did nature make you feel related to everything around you?
What question about meaning would you like to ask an older you?
When did you notice that your desires changed and what did that teach you?
What do you want to be remembered for in one sentence?
When did you feel that life had offered you a second chance?
What thought about time comforts rather than worries you?
When did you sense a rhythm to your life you hadn't seen before?
What small act makes you feel like you're living according to your values?
When did you feel your sense of wonder return after a long stretch?
What do you wonder about the connection between suffering and meaning?
When did you feel both afraid and strangely peaceful about the future?
What would you change in your daily life to feel more purposeful?
When did you accept uncertainty and find that it felt freeing?
What belief about 'enough' would you like to explore more deeply?
When did you feel the peculiar comfort of recognizing life isn't only about you?
What small kindness do you think matters most in the long run?
When did you notice that letting go created space for surprise?
What question about belonging would you ask someone who feels like family?
When did you feel the tension between wanting safety and wanting growth?
What does a meaningful day look like for you now?
When did you feel a quiet certainty about a life decision?
What do you think humans most need to remember about connectedness?
When did you realize the value of ordinary routines in giving life shape?
What small truth about mortality do you hold closely?
When did you feel awe at a creation or art that shifted your view?
What question would you ask someone who seems deeply content?
When did you experience transcendence in a mundane moment?
What would you like to tell your future grandchild about living well?
When did you notice that worry and hope can coexist in the same thought?
What do you think gives life meaning beyond achievements?
When did you feel grateful for being exactly where you are?
What question about the unknown would you explore with a close friend?
When did you feel the sweet ache of being alive and aware?
What practice would you adopt to live with more wonder?
When did you trust a small impulse and it led to something meaningful?
What do you imagine when you think about a life well-loved?
When did you sense the limit of your understanding and sit with it?
What small decision would make your life feel more aligned tomorrow?
When did you feel most humbled by the vastness of time or space?
What would you ask someone who has faced major life questions and found peace?
When did you feel a surprising clarity about what matters most?
What do you want to invest your time in so your life feels meaningful later?
When did you feel beauty and sorrow in the same breath and what was it?
What question about purpose could you ask yourself each morning?
When did you notice your fears shrinking when met with curiosity?
What small thing would you do to bring more presence into your days?
When did you feel connected to the idea that everything changes and that's okay?
What do you think would make your later decades feel content?
When did you feel that small acts of kindness were enough to matter?
What question would you ask the version of you who is ten years older?

What moment of awe would you like to create for yourself this year?

What do you hope to keep learning about life as you grow older?
When did you notice a simple truth that changed a daily habit?
What would you put at the center of a life you'd be proud of?
When did you feel more curiosity than certainty and what did it spark?
What question would you ask tonight to deepen your sense of meaning?
